headline: Survey at 36% completion with strong regional spread but critical scale readiness gap.
collection_status: 481 of 1,350 responses collected (35.6% of target). All 10 regions represented with Lindi, Mara, and Tanga leading (231 responses, 48% of total). Collection pace adequate but must accelerate in remaining 7 months to meet target.
key_findings: Teacher engagement strong: 260 KiuFunza teachers (54% of responses) indicates programme visibility among core users; head teachers represent 24%, showing leadership buy-in.
Expansion priority dominant: 175 respondents (36%) prioritize KiuFunza expansion; only 76 (16%) focus on strengthening existing work—signals demand outpaces capacity.
Critical readiness-satisfaction paradox: Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) but scale readiness low (2.8/5), indicating stakeholders want growth but perceive operational constraints.

Government alignment emerging: Feedback emphasizes policy integration and sustained government collaboration; 97 responses (20%) cite policy as priority, reflecting institutional sustainability concerns.
flags: Scale readiness at 2.8/5 is dangerously low for expansion plans—risk of quality degradation if growth outpaces systems readiness.
District and regional officials severely under-represented (19 responses from 8 district/regional roles); strategy decisions may lack bureaucratic input needed for policy uptake.
Songwe, Rukwa, and Singida lag significantly (76 responses combined vs. 231 from top 3 regions)—geographic imbalance risks skewed findings and uneven implementation signals.
recommendation: Immediately audit operational readiness across the 5 areas implicit in feedback (monitoring systems, teacher incentives, digital tracking, inter-stakeholder communication, government coordination). Launch targeted outreach to district and regional education officials in underrepresented regions to secure buy-in for scale before committing expansion resources.
